This creates inconsistent behavior: the same dataset could receive transformed or untransformed columns depending on which execution path is taken. The logic should either always transform upfront or ensure all paths maintain consistent state.",

But if the cached value is `True`, it will call the function. This means cache hits for ineligible organizations don't call `_should_apply_sample_weight_transform`, creating inconsistent behavior. If that function has side effects or if eligibility changes, this introduces a subtle correctness bug.",
      "suggestion": "Separate cache validation from eligibility logic: `if cached_result is not None: result = cached_result` then later `return result and _should_apply_sample_weight_transform(...)` to ensure the transform check always runs regardless of cache state, or document why skipping the transform check is safe."
